message:
chore(beads): record fcp-oauth/provider + enforce_invoke + fcp-audit clean-pass

Audited fcp-oauth/src/provider.rs (OAuth provider catalog, no crypto
surface), MeshNode::enforce_invoke_request (correct ordering: idempotency
→ COSE verify → holder proof → revocation, fails closed), fcp-audit
AuditEntry.follows (checked_add protects against u64::MAX overflow).

Two observations logged for possible follow-up (not patched): (1)
AuditEntryBuilder::build accepts empty strings for id/event_type/actor/
zone_id — existing test explicitly documents this as intentional; (2)
VerifyIssue::is_critical omits audit.timestamp_regression and zone
mismatches — designers chose Warn over Fail, a policy judgment rather
than a correctness bug.

No concrete bugs found — clean-pass confirmed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-2nvn","title":"[SC] E2E: supply-chain verification flow","description":"# [SC] E2E: supply-chain verification flow

## Goal
End-to-end script to validate supply-chain gating across verify/install/report.

## Script
- `e2e/supply_chain_verify.sh` with structured JSON logs.
- Steps:
  1) Install valid bundle -> verify success + evidence bundle ID.
  2) Attempt invalid bundle -> rejection with correct error code.
  3) Apply dev policy override -> allow install + log override.
  4) Generate report -> verify provenance fields present.

## Logging
- Emit JSON per step: {test, stage, bundle_id, result, error_code?, evidence_id?, duration_ms}.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Script passes in CI and produces deterministic evidence IDs.
- Decision receipts and evidence bundle IDs appear in logs.","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add evidence ledger fields with per-check log-odds contributions and explicit loss decision.
- Add anytime-valid risk control (e-process) for approvals/denials and record epsilon in receipts.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Compile policies to DAG/BDD and cache allow/deny results with LRU.
- Use bitset capability sets for fast subset checks.

I hardened ConnectorProcessRunner::request() to validate that connector responses are JSON objects using jsonrpc=2.0, echo the original request id, and contain exactly one of result or error. For block_direct_network = false with network_constraints present, Preview2 raw TCP hostcalls were being allowed via socket_addr_check using only port + IP deny checks. That bypassed hostname/SNI/SPKI policy because socket_addr_check only sees a resolved SocketAddr, not the original host intent. In practice, a policy like host_allow = ["api.example.com"] could permit a direct socket connect to an arbitrary public IP on the allowed port. I tightened the runtime so raw Preview2 sockets are enabled only for the subset of policies that are actually enforceable at SocketAddr granularity (wildcard or exact-IP policy with no SNI/SPKI/IP-literal restrictions), and I added regressions proving hostname-bound policy is rejected while exact-IP policy still works. The wavefront path allocated `solved: vec\![None; k]` but needs `vec\![None; l]` (L = K'+S+H intermediate symbols) because the dense solve phase assigns solutions for ALL columns including repair constraint columns K..L-1. Sequential decode() at line 1629 correctly uses `l`. Also fixed active_cols from (0..k) to (0..l) for consistency with the sequential path. Verification: 738 fcp-raptorq lib tests pass, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-03-31T03:54:38Z"},{"id":2186,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-2ot4f","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-03-31: Fixed critical supply chain bypass in fcp-registry (crates/fcp-registry/src/lib.rs:1368-1379). When trusted_builders policy was set but attestations had empty builder_id (or attestations vec was empty), the builder validation silently passed — allowing supply chain bypass. Fix requires at least one attestation to identify a trusted builder before accepting. Also rejects individual attestations with untrusted builders. Updated test supply_chain_trusted_builder_no_builder_id to expect rejection. Verification: 342 lib + 85 integration = 427 tests pass, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-03-31T20:12:06Z"},{"id":2194,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-2ot4f","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-03-31: Extended audit sweep results. fcp-crypto: clean — X25519 zero-point fix verified (x25519.rs:87-89), Ed25519 uses constant-time dalek, HPKE nonces delegated to hpke crate, MAC uses subtle::ConstantTimeEq, zero panics in production code. fcp-cbor: clean — RFC 8949 §4.2 canonical encoding correct, depth limit 128 enforced, 64MiB size limit, duplicate key detection, byte-for-byte round-trip verification. fcp-store: clean — GC/repair/coverage code uses saturating arithmetic, no concrete bugs. fcp-protocol: constant-time MAC (session.rs:909), proper nonce derivation, integer overflow checks in frame validation. Workspace verification: 3151 fcp-host tests pass, 770 fcp-store tests pass, 739 fcp-raptorq tests pass, 427 fcp-registry tests pass. All 42 modified files compile cleanly.","created_at":"2026-04-01T03:07:24Z"},{"id":2688,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-2ot4f","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-04-17: Fresh-eyes audit of fcp-webhook clean. Reviewed signature.rs (1013 lines) + handler.rs (1498 lines) + provider.rs (2263 lines, full GitHub/Stripe/Slack/Linear/Discord parsers). Findings: (1) Signature verification uses constant-time mac.verify_slice() across HmacSha256/HmacSha1/Ed25519 paths — OK. (2) Replay protection via atomic claim_event() under RwLock write, bounded MAX_SEEN_EVENTS=100_000 with proper cache-full guard (line 214 & 252), cleanup rate-limited to once per minute (line 271). (3) Secrets [REDACTED] in Debug output across all verifiers. (4) Stripe parser handles multiple v1 signatures, validates timestamp with abs_diff + tolerance window (line 255), verifies signed_payload=timestamp.body. (5) Slack v0:timestamp:body construction correct. (6) GitHub falls back to deterministic_event_id when x-github-delivery missing/empty. Minor observation (non-bug): deterministic_event_id_with_context uses 0-byte delimiters rather than length-prefixed encoding, so context=None and context=Some() produce identical hashes — this only affects idempotency cache granularity, not authentication, and is not exploitable. Clean pass.","created_at":"2026-04-17T05:33:10Z"},{"id":2689,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-2ot4f","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-04-17: Fresh-eyes audit of fcp-audit (crates/fcp-audit/src/lib.rs, 5029 lines) clean. Reviewed audit chain verification machinery: verify_chain (line 855) checks genesis seq=0/prev=None, monotonic seq without gaps (saturating_add), prev pointer matches predecessor id, timestamp regression detection (entry.occurred_at < prev.occurred_at), duplicate-seq fork detection via HashMap<u64,&str> (line 896-910), head/tip consistency, zone filter. No unsafe code (#![forbid(unsafe_code)]). AuditEntry.follows() uses checked_add(1). Severity mapping (Info/Warning/Error/Critical) deterministic via event_type matching. Observation: ChainHead::has_quorum() returns signature_count > 0 which only means 'any signature', not actual n-f threshold — real quorum policy enforcement lives elsewhere (fcp-core). meets_coverage() handles NaN defensively via >= semantics. Clean pass, no concrete bugs found.","created_at":"2026-04-17T05:34:21Z"},{"id":2690,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-2ot4f","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-04-17: Fresh-eyes audit of fcp-manifest (347KB lib.rs) + fcp-oauth (8419 lines across 7 files) found one defensive hardening issue — PATCHED. Issue: crates/fcp-oauth/src/token.rs OAuthTokens::from_response (line 117) and update_from_response (line 211) blindly accepted response.refresh_token and response.id_token without checking for empty string. A compromised/malicious OAuth server returning refresh_token: "" would permanently break the refresh loop by overwriting a valid refresh token with an empty string, forcing the user to re-authenticate from scratch. Same issue for id_token. Patched with .filter(|s| !s.is_empty()) in both locations. Added 2 regression tests: test_from_response_rejects_empty_refresh_token (empty string on construction drops to None) and test_update_from_response_rejects_empty_refresh_token (empty string on refresh preserves existing token). Remaining audits clean: fcp-manifest SignatureThreshold k-of-n validation correct, validate_connector_state_subpath rejects ../.. via Component::Normal matching, capability id lint patterns correct, fcp-oauth CallbackParams::validate uses subtle::ct_eq (constant-time), PKCE uses SHA256 with 32 bytes OsRng (RFC 7636 compliant). Verification: local cargo blocked by unrelated asupersync compile errors (61 upstream errors in /Users/jemanuel/projects/asupersync/); rch blocked by 188-root dependency sync loop. Patch is purely additive defensive (Option<String>::filter). Added tests are simple unit assertions.","created_at":"2026-04-17T05:49:00Z"},{"id":2691,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-2ot4f","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-04-17: Fresh-eyes audit of fcp-mesh iblt.rs found a latent robustness gap — PATCHED. Issue: crates/fcp-mesh/src/iblt.rs Iblt::with_cell_count() only rejected cell_count == 0, but cell_count in {1, 2} is also invalid because indices_for() uses IBLT_HASH_COUNT=3 hash positions per key via linear probing over the cell array. With cell_count < 3, the probing loop exhausts its budget (steps < cell_count) before finding a distinct slot, silently returning duplicate indices. A single insert would then double-apply to the same cell (count += 1 twice for one object) breaking the peeling invariant that decode() relies on. Patch: with_cell_count now rejects cell_count < IBLT_HASH_COUNT, converting the unit variant InvalidCellCount to a struct variant InvalidCellCount { got: usize } for better diagnostics. Added 2 regression tests: cell_count_below_hash_count_is_rejected (loops 1..IBLT_HASH_COUNT) and cell_count_equal_to_hash_count_is_accepted (proves the new lower bound works). Impact: currently not network-reachable because (a) all production callers use with_expected_difference which guarantees MIN_RECOMMENDED_IBLT_CELLS=64, and (b) Iblt is not yet deserialized from wire bytes (gossip uses IbltPlaceholder instead). Defensive hardening for future refactors. Additional observations (NOT bugs, not patched): IbltCell arithmetic uses += and - (not saturating); pure_key uses .abs() which would panic on i32::MIN in debug. Reachable only via directly-constructed adversarial cells, not network input at this time. Verification: local cargo + rch both blocked by pre-existing asupersync upstream compile errors (61 errors in /dp/asupersync/src/trace/refinement_firewall.rs and epoch_gc_integration.rs, same blocker reported throughout audit history). Patch itself is trivially correct — no external callers reference IbltError::InvalidCellCount (verified via grep).","created_at":"2026-04-17T06:02:41Z"},{"id":2692,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-2ot4f","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-04-17: Fresh-eyes audit of fcp-mesh/src/{authority,session,admission}.rs — clean pass. Reviewed: (1) authority.rs (461 lines) — AuthorityView::from_observed correctly sorts leases by descending (fencing_token, expires_at) ASC by holder, picks first active, marks rest superseded/expired. Minor observation: is_winner_record() compares 5 fields so genuinely-duplicate observed entries (same holder+subject+purpose+fencing_token+expires_at) would both mark Active — but this requires gossip duplicate delivery, not network-exploitable. (2) session.rs (1634 lines) + fcp-protocol/src/session.rs ReplayWindow — DTLS-style sliding bitmap (u128), seq==0 rejected, check() before MAC verification to prevent CPU-exhaustion replay-burn, check_and_update() after MAC passes to prevent window burn by forged frames. next_send_seq panics on u64::MAX to prevent nonce reuse. checked_shl with min(128) handles full-reset shifts safely. Comments explicitly document the security ordering. (3) admission.rs (2932 lines) — PeerBudget uses saturating_add/saturating_sub throughout, per-peer 1-minute windows with maybe_reset_window, anti-amplification factor saturates at u32::MAX (CAN'T under-protect, only over-restrict), try_acquire_decode/release_decode pair uses >= limit guard so u32 never overflows, gc_stale_peers retains peers with in-flight decodes OR recent activity. Design note: check_admission is check-only (record is separate), so under concurrent load budget enforcement is loose — this is standard TOCTOU-tolerant rate-limiting pattern, not a bug. No concrete bugs found in these 3 files.","created_at":"2026-04-17T06:06:46Z"},{"id":2693,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-2ot4f","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-04-17: Fresh-eyes audit of fcp-mesh/src/coordinator.rs found 2 panic-on-adversarial-input bugs — PATCHED. Issue 1: crates/fcp-mesh/src/coordinator.rs LeaseCoordinator::rebase_next_seq (line 538-549) used max_observed.checked_add(1).expect('fencing token space exhausted'). A malicious or buggy peer reporting an ObservedLeaseAuthority with fencing_token == u64::MAX would panic the coordinator on the next acquire() call, because rebase_next_seq runs before handing out a new token. This is a direct peer-induced DoS: any peer whose observed leases reach LeaseCoordinator::acquire() can crash it with one well-crafted message. Fix: saturating_add(1) instead of checked_add+expect, with a note that next_fencing_token() will still refuse to hand out the final token when next_seq reaches u64::MAX (deterministic own-exhaustion vs external DoS). Issue 2: LeaseCoordinator::should_renew (line 517) computed 'remaining * 10_000' as u64 where remaining was lease.expires_at.saturating_sub(now_secs). If expires_at is u64::MAX (adversarial input or sentinel misuse), remaining is near u64::MAX and the multiplication overflows. Debug: panic. Release: silent wrap producing wrong bps → unnecessary renewal. Fix: widen to u128 intermediate ((u128::from(remaining) * 10_000) / u128::from(reference_ttl)) and compare against u128::from(renew_threshold_bps). Added 2 regression tests: rebase_next_seq_does_not_panic_on_u64_max_fencing_token (exercises acquire() with poisoned lease) and should_renew_does_not_panic_on_u64_max_remaining. Verification pending upstream: rch still blocked by pre-existing asupersync compile errors. Patches are purely additive (defensive arithmetic replacements) — no external callers of rebase_next_seq (private fn) and should_renew signature unchanged.","created_at":"2026-04-17T06:11:20Z"},{"id":2694,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-2ot4f","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-04-17: Fresh-eyes audit of fcp-mesh/src/gossip.rs partial — architectural concern (NOT a concrete bug yet) logged for tracking. GossipMachine::handle_summary (line 1257) accepts GossipSummary and updates peer state based on summary.from, summary.object_filter_digest, etc., but does NOT verify summary.signature: Option<NodeSignature>. The struct doc says 'Signed gossip summary for anti-entropy (NORMATIVE)' and the signing_bytes() method + with_signature() attachment helper both exist, but handle_summary() never invokes verification. GossipRequest has the same pattern (line 728). Current reachability: handle_summary() is called only from tests (mesh_integration.rs, multi_node_harness.rs) and internal unit tests — NO production wire-transport call site. So the gap is latent: whoever wires the gossip transport will need to either (a) verify summary.signature before calling handle_summary, or (b) have handle_summary verify internally. Recommend option (b) since the struct DOC claims 'signed' (normative) and leaving verification to every caller is error-prone. Deferring the actual patch because it requires a key-lookup callback parameter that touches the MeshNode type — larger than a one-file defensive fix. handle_request (line 1409) is bounded correctly (max_objects/max_symbols limits, .take() bounds, filter guards on zone_state). Other gossip bound checks I spot-verified: handle_summary checks staleness + object_count/symbol_count caps + max_iblt_bytes (via IbltPlaceholder::decode_with_limits). Budget enforcement looks solid; auth enforcement is the gap.","created_at":"2026-04-17T06:14:36Z"},{"id":2695,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-2ot4f","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-04-17: Fresh-eyes audit of fcp-mesh/src/symbol_request.rs found a defensive gap — PATCHED. Issue: SymbolRequestHandler::process_symbol_ack (line 401) unconditionally inserted ack.object_id into self.completed_transfers (an unbounded HashMap) regardless of whether the object_id was ever in active_transfers or completed_awaiting_ack. A forged SymbolAck from an unauthenticated peer (or any caller that bypasses signature verification — which the current code does NOT perform — see node.rs:1215 handle_symbol_ack) could thus (a) pollute completed_transfers between prune_stale_state cycles and (b) cause should_stop() to lie about transfer state for an object ID we never actually served. Patch: process_symbol_ack now tracks whether the object was known (was_awaiting_ack || had_active) and drops the ack with a warn! log if unknown. Updated 2 existing tests: process_symbol_ack_unknown_object_is_noop → process_symbol_ack_unknown_object_is_dropped (now asserts should_stop returns false for the unknown id); prune_stale_state_removes_completed_transfers now calls track_transfer first to establish a real transfer before the ack (since the old version was relying on the buggy behavior to populate completed_transfers). Added explicit doc note on process_symbol_ack warning callers to verify ack.signature before invocation. Other process_symbol_ack tests already call track_transfer first (verified lines 1187, 1890, 2378). Separate architectural finding: MeshNode::handle_symbol_ack (node.rs:1215) delegates to process_symbol_ack without verifying ack.signature, despite SymbolAck's signature field being mandatory (non-Option) Ed25519Signature and SymbolAck::verify(&VerifyingKey) existing in fcp-protocol. Same pattern as the gossip handle_summary gap I noted earlier — production wire transport not yet reached, but gap is latent. Verification pending upstream: rch + local both blocked by asupersync compile errors (pre-existing).","created_at":"2026-04-17T06:21:18Z"},{"id":2696,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-2ot4f","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-04-17: Same defensive gap class in process_decode_status — PATCHED. Issue: SymbolRequestHandler::process_decode_status (line 348) unconditionally inserted status.object_id into completed_awaiting_ack when status.complete=true, even for unknown objects. A forged DecodeStatus{complete: true} from an unauthenticated peer could fill completed_awaiting_ack unboundedly between prune cycles. The DecodeStatus struct also has a mandatory signature field but MeshNode::handle_decode_status (node.rs:1210) doesn't verify it (same pattern as handle_symbol_ack). Patch: restructured process_decode_status to early-return when object_id is unknown (let-else on active_transfers.get_mut), with a warn! log if the dropped status claimed complete=true. All state writes (last_status, last_activity, completed_awaiting_ack insert, stopped flag) now gated on 'we actually have this transfer'. Existing tests compatible: process_decode_status_unknown_object_is_noop (complete=false, early return, active_count stays 0 ✓); prune_stale_state_removes_completed_awaiting_ack + process_decode_status_incomplete_updates_state already call track_transfer first ✓. Combined with earlier process_symbol_ack fix, the handler now properly rejects ALL peer-sourced state pollution for unknown object_ids. Verification pending upstream (asupersync blocker).","created_at":"2026-04-17T06:24:07Z"},{"id":2697,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-2ot4f","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-04-17: Fresh-eyes audit of fcp-mesh/src/degraded.rs found an unbounded memory growth vector — PATCHED. Issue: DegradedModeDecoder.pending (HashMap<PendingReconstructionKey, PendingReconstruction>) was completely unbounded. PendingReconstructionKey combines object_id (32 bytes), zone_id_hash (32 bytes), zone_key_id, epoch_id (u64 — attacker-controlled), symbol_size, source_symbols_k. Each PendingReconstruction holds a full RaptorQDecoder (multi-KiB). An attacker sending FCPS control-plane frames with unique (object_id, epoch_id) tuples could create arbitrary many pending entries → classic memory-exhaustion DoS. DegradedModeDecoder::process_frame (line 347) is reachable via MeshNode::decode_control_plane (node.rs:1269) which is reachable via process_control_plane_frame (node.rs:1286) — NO upstream signature verification (process_signed_frame takes a different path). Patch: added DEFAULT_MAX_PENDING_RECONSTRUCTIONS=256 constant, DegradedModeDecoder now tracks max_pending, new with_max_pending(config, max) constructor, DegradedModeDecoder::new preserves signature and defaults to 256. process_frame rejects NEW pending entries (entries not already in the map) once pending.len() >= max_pending, returning new PendingLimitExceeded { current, limit } variant. Adding symbols to EXISTING reconstructions still works at cap (progress, not allocation). Added regression test decoder_rejects_new_pending_beyond_cap with manually-constructed K=4/1-symbol frames hitting cap=2. All existing callers use DegradedModeDecoder::new(config) → automatically get default cap. Verification pending upstream (asupersync compile blocker).","created_at":"2026-04-17T06:32:42Z"},{"id":2698,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-2ot4f","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-04-17: Additional fresh-eyes audit clean-pass on fcp-mesh/src/device.rs + fcp-mesh/src/planner.rs + fcp-oauth/src/oauth1.rs + OperationSection validation in fcp-manifest. Reviewed: (1) device.rs (2981 lines) — purely device profile/fitness scoring (GpuProfile, TpuProfile, DeviceProfile, FitnessScore, HRW ranking context). No signatures, no network decode, no crypto; not a security-audit target. (2) planner.rs plan() + score_node() + check_lease_constraints (line 470-669) — deterministic sort, per-node scoring, hard-constraint filters (connector missing, version mismatch, lease conflict). Only notable observation: version_gte() (line 856) uses s.split(.).filter_map(parse::<u32>().ok()) which silently drops non-numeric segments, so e.g. "1.2-foo.99" parses as [1, 99] and could accidentally satisfy required "1.2.3" by comparing 99 > 2 at index 1. But versions here are self-reported by nodes, so any comparison mis-interpretation only affects nodes that either self-report truthfully (normal versions work) or already-adversarial nodes (which can lie about anything anyway). Not a new attack vector. (3) oauth1.rs calculate_signature (line 344) — RFC 5849-compliant HMAC-SHA1 signing, correct URL normalization, parameter sorting, percent-encoding, signing key = consumer_secret AND token_secret. Outgoing-only (client signs its own requests), no inbound verification so no constant-time concern. (4) fcp-manifest OperationSection::validate (line 1466) — validates description non-empty, recursively validates rate_limit and network_constraints. input_schema/output_schema are serde_json::Value but canonical CBOR encoding in compute_interface_hash enforces the fcp-cbor 128-level depth limit + 64 MiB size cap per my earlier fcp-audit pass, so pathological JSON cannot DoS interface hashing. Runtime JSON schema validation is outside this crate surface. Clean pass — no bugs to patch in this iteration.","created_at":"2026-04-17T06:36:00Z"}]}

Issue: crates/fcp-mesh/src/iblt.rs Iblt::with_cell_count() only rejected cell_count == 0, but cell_count in {1, 2} is also invalid because indices_for() uses IBLT_HASH_COUNT=3 hash positions per key via linear probing over the cell array. With cell_count < 3, the probing loop exhausts its budget (steps < cell_count) before finding a distinct slot, silently returning duplicate indices. A single insert would then double-apply to the same cell (count += 1 twice for one object) breaking the peeling invariant that decode() relies on. Patch: with_cell_count now rejects cell_count < IBLT_HASH_COUNT, converting the unit variant InvalidCellCount to a struct variant InvalidCellCount { got: usize } for better diagnostics. Added 2 regression tests: cell_count_below_hash_count_is_rejected (loops 1..IBLT_HASH_COUNT) and cell_count_equal_to_hash_count_is_accepted (proves the new lower bound works). Impact: currently not network-reachable because (a) all production callers use with_expected_difference which guarantees MIN_RECOMMENDED_IBLT_CELLS=64, and (b) Iblt is not yet deserialized from wire bytes (gossip uses IbltPlaceholder instead). Defensive hardening for future refactors. Issue 1: crates/fcp-mesh/src/coordinator.rs LeaseCoordinator::rebase_next_seq (line 538-549) used max_observed.checked_add(1).expect('fencing token space exhausted'). A malicious or buggy peer reporting an ObservedLeaseAuthority with fencing_token == u64::MAX would panic the coordinator on the next acquire() call, because rebase_next_seq runs before handing out a new token. This is a direct peer-induced DoS: any peer whose observed leases reach LeaseCoordinator::acquire() can crash it with one well-crafted message. Fix: saturating_add(1) instead of checked_add+expect, with a note that next_fencing_token() will still refuse to hand out the final token when next_seq reaches u64::MAX (deterministic own-exhaustion vs external DoS). Issue 2: LeaseCoordinator::should_renew (line 517) computed 'remaining * 10_000' as u64 where remaining was lease.expires_at.saturating_sub(now_secs). If expires_at is u64::MAX (adversarial input or sentinel misuse), remaining is near u64::MAX and the multiplication overflows. Debug: panic. Release: silent wrap producing wrong bps → unnecessary renewal. Fix: widen to u128 intermediate ((u128::from(remaining) * 10_000) / u128::from(reference_ttl)) and compare against u128::from(renew_threshold_bps). Added 2 regression tests: rebase_next_seq_does_not_panic_on_u64_max_fencing_token (exercises acquire() with poisoned lease) and should_renew_does_not_panic_on_u64_max_remaining. Verification pending upstream: rch still blocked by pre-existing asupersync compile errors. GossipRequest has the same pattern (line 728). Current reachability: handle_summary() is called only from tests (mesh_integration.rs, multi_node_harness.rs) and internal unit tests — NO production wire-transport call site. So the gap is latent: whoever wires the gossip transport will need to either (a) verify summary.signature before calling handle_summary, or (b) have handle_summary verify internally. Recommend option (b) since the struct DOC claims 'signed' (normative) and leaving verification to every caller is error-prone. Issue: SymbolRequestHandler::process_symbol_ack (line 401) unconditionally inserted ack.object_id into self.completed_transfers (an unbounded HashMap) regardless of whether the object_id was ever in active_transfers or completed_awaiting_ack. A forged SymbolAck from an unauthenticated peer (or any caller that bypasses signature verification — which the current code does NOT perform — see node.rs:1215 handle_symbol_ack) could thus (a) pollute completed_transfers between prune_stale_state cycles and (b) cause should_stop() to lie about transfer state for an object ID we never actually served. Patch: process_symbol_ack now tracks whether the object was known (was_awaiting_ack || had_active) and drops the ack with a warn! log if unknown. Updated 2 existing tests: process_symbol_ack_unknown_object_is_noop → process_symbol_ack_unknown_object_is_dropped (now asserts should_stop returns false for the unknown id); prune_stale_state_removes_completed_transfers now calls track_transfer first to establish a real transfer before the ack (since the old version was relying on the buggy behavior to populate completed_transfers). Added explicit doc note on process_symbol_ack warning callers to verify ack.signature before invocation. Other process_symbol_ack tests already call track_transfer first (verified lines 1187, 1890, 2378). Separate architectural finding: MeshNode::handle_symbol_ack (node.rs:1215) delegates to process_symbol_ack without verifying ack.signature, despite SymbolAck's signature field being mandatory (non-Option) Ed25519Signature and SymbolAck::verify(&VerifyingKey) existing in fcp-protocol. A forged DecodeStatus{complete: true} from an unauthenticated peer could fill completed_awaiting_ack unboundedly between prune cycles. The DecodeStatus struct also has a mandatory signature field but MeshNode::handle_decode_status (node.rs:1210) doesn't verify it (same pattern as handle_symbol_ack). Patch: restructured process_decode_status to early-return when object_id is unknown (let-else on active_transfers.get_mut), with a warn! log if the dropped status claimed complete=true. Issue: DegradedModeDecoder.pending (HashMap<PendingReconstructionKey, PendingReconstruction>) was completely unbounded. PendingReconstructionKey combines object_id (32 bytes), zone_id_hash (32 bytes), zone_key_id, epoch_id (u64 — attacker-controlled), symbol_size, source_symbols_k. Each PendingReconstruction holds a full RaptorQDecoder (multi-KiB). An attacker sending FCPS control-plane frames with unique (object_id, epoch_id) tuples could create arbitrary many pending entries → classic memory-exhaustion DoS. DegradedModeDecoder::process_frame (line 347) is reachable via MeshNode::decode_control_plane (node.rs:1269) which is reachable via process_control_plane_frame (node.rs:1286) — NO upstream signature verification (process_signed_frame takes a different path). Patch: added DEFAULT_MAX_PENDING_RECONSTRUCTIONS=256 constant, DegradedModeDecoder now tracks max_pending, new with_max_pending(config, max) constructor, DegradedModeDecoder::new preserves signature and defaults to 256. process_frame rejects NEW pending entries (entries not already in the map) once pending.len() >= max_pending, returning new PendingLimitExceeded { current, limit } variant. Adding symbols to EXISTING reconstructions still works at cap (progress, not allocation). Added regression test decoder_rejects_new_pending_beyond_cap with manually-constructed K=4/1-symbol frames hitting cap=2. All existing callers use DegradedModeDecoder::new(config) → automatically get default cap. Verification pending upstream (asupersync compile blocker).","created_at":"2026-04-17T06:32:42Z"},{"id":2698,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-2ot4f","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-04-17: Additional fresh-eyes audit clean-pass on fcp-mesh/src/device.rs + fcp-mesh/src/planner.rs + fcp-oauth/src/oauth1.rs + OperationSection validation in fcp-manifest. Reviewed: (1) device.rs (2981 lines) — purely device profile/fitness scoring (GpuProfile, TpuProfile, DeviceProfile, FitnessScore, HRW ranking context). No signatures, no network decode, no crypto; not a security-audit target. (2) planner.rs plan() + score_node() + check_lease_constraints (line 470-669) — deterministic sort, per-node scoring, hard-constraint filters (connector missing, version mismatch, lease conflict). Only notable observation: version_gte() (line 856) uses s.split(.).filter_map(parse::<u32>().ok()) which silently drops non-numeric segments, so e.g. "1.2-foo.99" parses as [1, 99] and could accidentally satisfy required "1.2.3" by comparing 99 > 2 at index 1. But versions here are self-reported by nodes, so any comparison mis-interpretation only affects nodes that either self-report truthfully (normal versions work) or already-adversarial nodes (which can lie about anything anyway). Not a new attack vector. (3) oauth1.rs calculate_signature (line 344) — RFC 5849-compliant HMAC-SHA1 signing, correct URL normalization, parameter sorting, percent-encoding, signing key = consumer_secret AND token_secret. Outgoing-only (client signs its own requests), no inbound verification so no constant-time concern. (4) fcp-manifest OperationSection::validate (line 1466) — validates description non-empty, recursively validates rate_limit and network_constraints. input_schema/output_schema are serde_json::Value but canonical CBOR encoding in compute_interface_hash enforces the fcp-cbor 128-level depth limit + 64 MiB size cap per my earlier fcp-audit pass, so pathological JSON cannot DoS interface hashing.  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-2rnf","title":"[FCP2-SDK] Rate Limit Declarations: SDK Trait, Pool Config, Tool Mapping","description":"### Rate Limit Declaration (SDK Trait Extension)

Connectors MUST declare their rate limits so agents can plan operations:

```rust
#[async_trait]
pub trait FcpConnector {
    // ... existing methods ...

    /// Return connector metadata consistent with manifest
    fn metadata(&self) -> Connector;

    /// Return MCP-compatible tool descriptors
    fn tool_descriptors(&self) -> Vec<ToolDescriptor>;

    /// **NEW:** Declare rate limits that apply to this connector's operations
    fn rate_limits(&self) -> RateLimitDeclarations {
        // Default: no rate limits declared (connectors SHOULD override)
        RateLimitDeclarations::default()
    }

    /// Handle control-plane objects by SchemaId dispatch
    async fn handle_control_plane(
        &self,
        obj: ControlPlaneObject
    ) -> Result<ControlPlaneObject, FcpError>;
}

/// Rate limit declarations for a connector
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct RateLimitDeclarations {
    /// Named rate limit pools
    pub limits: Vec<RateLimitPool>,
    /// Which tools consume from which pools
    pub tool_pool_map: HashMap<String, Vec<String>>,
}

/// A named rate limit pool
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct RateLimitPool {
    /// Unique identifier for this limit (e.g., "discord_api", "openai_tokens")
    pub id: String,
    /// Human-readable description
    pub description: String,
    /// Rate limit configuration
    pub config: RateLimitConfig,
    /// Whether this is a hard limit (blocks) or soft limit (warns)
    pub enforcement: RateLimitEnforcement,
    /// Whether limit is shared across connector instances
    pub scope: RateLimitScope,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct RateLimitConfig {
    /// Maximum requests per window
    pub requests: u32,
    /// Window duration
    pub window: Duration,
    /// Burst allowance (optional, for token bucket)
    pub burst: Option<u32>,
    /// Unit of measurement (requests, tokens, bytes)
    pub unit: RateLimitUnit,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub enum RateLimitUnit {
    /// Number of API requests
    Requests,
    /// Tokens (for LLM APIs)
    Tokens,
    /// Bytes transferred
    Bytes,
    /// Custom unit (connector-specific)
    Custom,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub enum RateLimitEnforcement {
    /// Block operations that would exceed limit
    Hard,
    /// Allow but emit warning metrics
    Soft,
    /// Advisory only (for external limits we can't enforce)
    Advisory,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub enum RateLimitScope {
    /// Per-connector-instance
    Instance,
    /// Per-credential (API key)
    Credential,
    /// Global across all instances
    Global,
}
```

### Example: Discord Connector Rate Limits

```rust
impl FcpConnector for DiscordConnector {
    fn rate_limits(&self) -> RateLimitDeclarations {
        RateLimitDeclarations {
            limits: vec![
                RateLimitPool {
                    id: "discord_api".to_string(),
                    description: "Discord REST API global rate limit".to_string(),
                    config: RateLimitConfig {
                        requests: 50,
                        window: Duration::from_secs(1),
                        burst: Some(100),
                        unit: RateLimitUnit::Requests,
                    },
                    enforcement: RateLimitEnforcement::Hard,
                    scope: RateLimitScope::Credential,
                },
                RateLimitPool {
                    id: "discord_messages".to_string(),
                    description: "Per-channel message rate limit".to_string(),
                    config: RateLimitConfig {
                        requests: 5,
                        window: Duration::from_secs(5),
                        burst: None,
                        unit: RateLimitUnit::Requests,
                    },
                    enforcement: RateLimitEnforcement::Hard,
                    scope: RateLimitScope::Instance, // Per channel
                },
            ],
            tool_pool_map: [
                ("send_message".to_string(), vec!["discord_api".to_string(), "discord_messages".to_string()]),
                ("send_dm".to_string(), vec!["discord_api".to_string()]),
                ("list_channels".to_string(), vec!["discord_api".to_string()]),
                ("get_message".to_string(), vec!["discord_api".to_string()]),
            ].into_iter().collect(),
        }
    }
}

### Host-Side Rate Limit Aggregation

The `fcp-host` aggregates rate limit declarations from all connectors:

```rust
impl ConnectorRegistry {
    /// Get unified rate limit view across all connectors
    pub fn aggregate_rate_limits(&self) -> AggregatedRateLimits {
        let mut limits = Vec::new();
        for connector in &self.connectors {
            let decls = connector.rate_limits();
            for pool in decls.limits {
                limits.push(RateLimitInfo {
                    connector_id: connector.id.clone(),
                    pool,
                    tools: decls.tool_pool_map.iter()
                        .filter(|(_, pools)| pools.contains(&pool.id))
                        .map(|(tool, _)| format!("{}.{}", connector.id, tool))
                        .collect(),
                });
            }
        }
        AggregatedRateLimits { limits }
    }
}
```

### Rate Limit Tests

```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod rate_limit_tests {
    #[test]
    fn test_rate_limit_declaration_complete() {
        let connector = DiscordConnector::new();
        let decls = connector.rate_limits();

        // Every tool should map to at least one pool
        for tool in connector.tool_descriptors() {
            assert!(
                decls.tool_pool_map.contains_key(&tool.name),
                "Tool {} missing rate limit mapping", tool.name
            );
        }

        // Every referenced pool should exist
        for pools in decls.tool_pool_map.values() {
            for pool_id in pools {
                assert!(
                    decls.limits.iter().any(|p| &p.id == pool_id),
                    "Pool {} referenced but not declared", pool_id
                );
            }
        }
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_rate_limit_serialization_roundtrip() {
        let decls = RateLimitDeclarations {
            limits: vec![RateLimitPool {
                id: "test".to_string(),
                description: "Test pool".to_string(),
                config: RateLimitConfig {
                    requests: 100,
                    window: Duration::from_secs(60),
                    burst: Some(150),
                    unit: RateLimitUnit::Requests,
                },
                enforcement: RateLimitEnforcement::Hard,
                scope: RateLimitScope::Credential,
            }],
            tool_pool_map: [("tool1".to_string(), vec!["test".to_string()])].into_iter().collect(),
        };

        let json = serde_json::to_string(&decls).unwrap();
        let parsed: RateLimitDeclarations = serde_json::from_str(&json).unwrap();

        assert_eq!(decls.limits.len(), parsed.limits.len());
        assert_eq!(decls.limits[0].config.requests, parsed.limits[0].config.requests);
    }
}
